name: Indiana Dietitian Certification
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.streetAddress:
402 West Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204
address.description: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, 402 W Washington Street, Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by Dec 31, even years) + 30 CE hours

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from the university, showing degree in an approved field and date awarded.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.

requiredDocuments.name: Two Passport-Style Photographs
requiredDocuments.description: Recent photographs meeting standard passport specifications.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Indiana dietitian certification cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is only $20 — one of the lowest in the country. The biennial renewal is also $20 (plus a $10 late fee if submitted after December 31). Fingerprint background check fees are paid separately to the vendor.

mainEntity.name: Is Indiana dietitian certification required to practice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Indiana's Certified Dietitian (CD) credential is technically optional for practicing as a Registered Dietitian (RD). However, most employers in Indiana require both the national RD credential and the state CD certification.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education is required in Indiana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Indiana requires 30 hours of continuing education during each two-year licensure period for active renewal. Continuing education is not required for inactive renewal.

mainEntity.name: Does Indiana have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Indiana. To work in Indiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Indiana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
